topic: 2028 Democratic nominee candidate poll and market watch
source_note: Seed snapshot transcribed from the image text shared in Discord #the-tide-pool. Corrected after audit: the source columns are Candidate / Markets / Polling / Read. V2 stores vibes ingredients and derives the displayed bar from weighted component scores. Catalyst notes added 2026-05-13 from Tide Pool discussion; they are qualitative watchpoints, not probability updates. V3 adds a manual mention-signal capture shape: good/bad/neutral/unclear counts, previous-week comparison, source mix, and example refs. Twitter/X is intentionally excluded unless a usable source is explicitly provided. V4 presents catalysts as a calendar/timeline surface with manual refresh cadence; scheduled automation is intentionally not wired yet. V5 adds a separate Republican tab with seeded GOP rows from current public polling/market references; GOP fields are intentionally marked lower-confidence where markets and polls disagree. V6 adds a Republican-only Trump endorsement chance field. V7 shifts that field away from polling weight and toward Trump relationship, loyalty, vindictiveness, surrogate behavior, and permission-structure evidence. V8 adds Democratic early-state calendar tracking for the live Midwest/South applicant pool with candidate boost/downgrade reads. V9 adds Stephen A. Smith as a Democratic long-shot media-celebrity row. V10 records the active Vibes weight profile and formula version so future snapshots can distinguish hope-cycle and referendum-cycle views.

rows.note: CA gov, shadow campaign, has network and timing; coastal-progressive brand risk.
rows.vibes_rationale: Technically skilled, but the polish cracks under sustained exposure and the positive story reads PR-shaped.
rows.evidence_notes: California governing record; extensive national media exposure; shadow-campaign signals.
rows.liability_notes: CA machine-polish; coastal-progressive brand risk; scripted affect under sustained exposure.
rows.catalyst_note: National Dem elite signals, California succession politics, the 2026 California governor result, and any post-governor launch after January 2027.

rows.note: Leads polling on name ID; markets discount donor comeback risk less than operatives do.
rows.vibes_rationale: High name ID, but the public style still reads scripted, institutional, and backward-linked to 2024.
rows.evidence_notes: 2020 and 2024 national campaign record; VP record; high name ID polling.
rows.liability_notes: 2024 loss; donor skepticism; scripted institutional style; backward-looking brand.
rows.catalyst_note: Explicit run/pass signal, donor re-entry, and signs that party elites are willing to relitigate 2024 instead of moving on.

rows.note: Obama-tier grassroots energy; general-election ceiling is the priced risk.
rows.vibes_rationale: Authentic, materially specific, and strong at fight-picking; the positive national vision is the weaker leg.
rows.evidence_notes: Long-form interviews; grassroots fundraising; clear issue advocacy and hostile-media reps.
rows.liability_notes: General-election ceiling concern; positive national unity story less proven than combat/story specificity.
rows.catalyst_note: Progressive primary wins in 2026, grassroots fundraising shocks, and whether electability panic fades after midterm lessons settle.

rows.note: PA gov, disciplined Rust Belt messenger; 2026 reelection comes first.
rows.vibes_rationale: Competent patriotic-repair lane with Rust Belt discipline; warmth and long-form ease are less proven nationally.
rows.evidence_notes: Pennsylvania executive record; disciplined Rust Belt messaging; crisis response profile.
rows.liability_notes: 2026 reelection gate; national long-form warmth less proven; Gaza/education fights may complicate primary.
rows.catalyst_note: Pennsylvania reelection margin on Nov 3, 2026; a strong swing-state overperformance is the cleanest repricing event.

rows.note: High measured favorability and communication skill; no governor track record or structural base.
rows.vibes_rationale: Probably the cleanest communicator for optimistic repair and long-form normie persuasion.
rows.evidence_notes: Long-form interviews; Fox/news hits; high measured favorability where tested.
rows.liability_notes: No governor track record; weak structural base; 2020 coalition ceiling still a question.
rows.catalyst_note: DNC early-state calendar, New Hampshire-style favorability tests, early-state polling, and whether he finds an office-holder story.

rows.note: IL gov, deep pockets, anti-Trump fighter posture; could buy into top tier on populist economics.
rows.vibes_rationale: Has fight-picking and material Illinois delivery; authenticity has improved, though the billionaire frame still drags.
rows.evidence_notes: Illinois governing record; economic-populist messaging; anti-Trump fight posture.
rows.liability_notes: Billionaire frame; can read over-managed; national voter familiarity limited.
rows.catalyst_note: Illinois reelection margin, anti-Trump donor spending, and whether money plus combat becomes the party lane after 2026.

rows.note: Georgia-incumbent story depends on Senate reelection first.
rows.vibes_rationale: Calm and polished, but the national story and unscripted emotional texture are still thin.
rows.evidence_notes: Georgia Senate record; calm media presence; incumbent swing-state story.
rows.liability_notes: Must win reelection; national story thin; limited unscripted emotional texture.
rows.catalyst_note: Georgia Senate path: May 19, 2026 primary, Nov 3, 2026 general. Win keeps him live; loss probably ends the 2028 case.

rows.note: MD gov, Rhodes scholar, combat vet; likely underpriced if markets omit him.
rows.vibes_rationale: Service biography, tone, and material story fit the thesis; long-form proof is thinner, but the signals are strong.
rows.evidence_notes: Maryland executive record; service biography; memoir/public speaking profile.
rows.liability_notes: Limited national testing; long-form proof thinner than the biography; early hype risk.
rows.catalyst_note: National rollout, early-state visits, service/reform speeches, and insider signs that the biography is being treated as presidential rather than just impressive.

rows.note: Two-term KY gov, southern, religious, moderate; fits the post-2024 candidate-shape argument.
rows.vibes_rationale: Red-state crisis governing gives him competence, decency, specificity, and low-key authentic hope.
rows.evidence_notes: Two-term Kentucky governing record; disaster response; red-state reelection proof.
rows.liability_notes: Low national exposure; moderate lane may blur in a primary; national fight selection untested.
rows.catalyst_note: Post-midterm lesson-taking around red-state Democratic overperformance, plus early-state travel or donor signals that his moderation has a lane.

rows.note: AZ senator, Marine combat vet, Latino, strong 2024 ticket-split overperformance.
rows.vibes_rationale: Marine vet origin story, swing-state proof, and fight-without-ideology coding make the profile unusually complete.
rows.evidence_notes: Arizona Senate win; Marine biography; ticket-split overperformance.
rows.liability_notes: Limited national testing; Senate record still new; inferred more from profile than campaign exposure.
rows.catalyst_note: Sun Belt and ticket-split lessons after 2026, veteran/Latino swing-state messaging, and any donor or early-state interest treating him as real.

rows.note: NC gov, won big while Harris lost the state; serious technocrat profile.
rows.vibes_rationale: Competence-and-AG-fight profile fits, but long-form national self and positive vision are still untested.
rows.evidence_notes: North Carolina win; attorney-general record; technocratic executive profile.
rows.liability_notes: Low national profile; positive vision and long-form persona mostly untested.
rows.catalyst_note: North Carolina performance as a party lesson, technocratic competence becoming fashionable, and early-state visits that raise his national profile.

rows.note: AZ senator, ex-astronaut, ex-Navy, Giffords; clean competent-adult template, low ambition signal.
rows.vibes_rationale: Competent patriotic adult template with broad appeal, but he has not shown much appetite for narrative politics.
rows.evidence_notes: Arizona Senate record; Navy/astronaut biography; broad competence brand.
rows.liability_notes: Low ambition signal; narrative politics not a strength; restrained affect may not travel.
rows.catalyst_note: Any ambition signal, VP/speculation cycle, Sun Belt security-and-competence lane, or early-state polling that rewards the competent-adult profile.

rows.note: GA senator, Black pastor, won Georgia three times in four years.
rows.vibes_rationale: Natural moral-patriotic storyteller who can name pain without sounding small or synthetic.
rows.evidence_notes: Georgia statewide wins; pastoral speaking style; moral-patriotic campaign message.
rows.liability_notes: National-primary appetite unknown; Senate lane may not translate; market omission risk.
rows.catalyst_note: Southern-state and Georgia lessons after 2026, moral-patriotic speeches, donor interest, and any early-state rollout showing he wants the lane.

rows.note: MI gov, strong Electoral College math; stock has slipped and gender handwringing weighs on the read.
rows.vibes_rationale: Lived threat, executive record, and fight-picking are real; the story has not fully translated beyond Michigan.
rows.evidence_notes: Michigan executive record; post-kidnapping threat history; national surrogate role.
rows.liability_notes: Stock has slipped; gender handwringing; story not fully translated beyond Michigan.
rows.catalyst_note: Michigan 2026 results, whether Michigan keeps early-calendar leverage, and whether she turns post-governor status into a future-party tour.

rows.note: MI senator, ex-CIA, security-credentialed moderate; pure dark horse.
rows.vibes_rationale: CIA-pragmatist-moderate profile is authentic and materially specific, but the positive vision is still unfinished.
rows.evidence_notes: Michigan Senate win; national-security biography; moderate pragmatist brand.
rows.liability_notes: Pure dark horse; positive vision unfinished; limited national charisma testing.
rows.catalyst_note: Michigan calendar leverage, national-security moderate demand, and any 2027 rollout that turns her from profile-fit dark horse into actual contender.

rows.note: CT senator with aggressive post-2024 fight-harder messaging.
rows.vibes_rationale: Fight-picking is strong, but the positive story is weak and the style can read sermonic.
rows.evidence_notes: Post-2024 fight-harder messaging; Senate issue advocacy; long-running gun-safety profile.
rows.liability_notes: Can sound sermonic; positive story lags fight posture; New England senator lane.
rows.catalyst_note: A viral fight or policy speech, post-2024 fight-harder tour momentum, exploratory/PAC moves, and proof the style can become a positive story.

rows.note: CO gov, term-limited in 2026, anti-orthodoxy moderate-libertarian Democrat, out gay.
rows.vibes_rationale: Independent, relaxed, and anti-orthodox enough for long-form appeal; national emotional story is fuzzy.
rows.evidence_notes: Colorado executive record; anti-orthodoxy positioning; relaxed media style.
rows.liability_notes: National emotional story fuzzy; libertarian-moderate profile may confuse primary voters.
rows.catalyst_note: Post-governor 2027 availability, anti-orthodoxy media moments, and whether a moderate-libertarian Dem lane looks useful rather than niche.

rows.note: HI senator, smart progressive policy profile, low national profile.
rows.vibes_rationale: Smart and humane policy voice, but low-profile Senate affect does not yet carry the thesis.
rows.evidence_notes: Senate policy work; smart progressive voice; climate/policy credibility.
rows.liability_notes: Low national profile; Senate affect; limited proof of campaign-stage magnetism.
rows.catalyst_note: A climate or governance policy moment, future-party speech, early-state travel, or viral long-form appearance that lifts him out of Senate-low-profile mode.

rows.note: Filibuster-record protest moment got attention; 2020 ceiling may still bind.
rows.vibes_rationale: Positive-hopeful language is real but overplayed enough to feel rehearsed; fight-picking can turn theatrical.
rows.evidence_notes: 2020 campaign; Senate record; filibuster protest visibility.
rows.liability_notes: Hope language can feel over-rehearsed; 2020 ceiling; fight-picking can turn theatrical.
rows.catalyst_note: Future-party speech, another high-visibility protest moment, early-state polling, and whether hopeful language feels less rehearsed this time.

rows.note: MN gov, 2024 VP nominee; bench cred but the loss drags on ceiling.
rows.vibes_rationale: Natural long-form performer with normal affect and a real vernacular, though 2024 makes the story contested.
rows.evidence_notes: Minnesota governing record; 2024 VP run; natural long-form/vernacular media style.
rows.liability_notes: 2024 loss overhang; VP brand tied to failed ticket; national story contested.
rows.catalyst_note: The 2024 loss fading, Midwest calendar leverage, strong long-form appearances, and whether the party wants normal vernacular affect again.

rows.note: Former NC gov, two-term swing-state executive, awkward post-office timeline.
rows.vibes_rationale: Pleasant and competent, but the story is not distinctive enough yet and long-form would likely be fine rather than magnetic.
rows.evidence_notes: Two-term North Carolina governorship; swing-state executive record.
rows.liability_notes: Post-office timing; low distinctiveness; long-form likely fine rather than magnetic.
rows.catalyst_note: Post-office rollout, North Carolina or Southern electability lesson-taking, donor meetings, and a clearer why-now story.

rows.note: Businessman-celebrity outsider lane; credible for the genre but still longshot.
rows.vibes_rationale: Excellent long-form authenticity and material business language, but office ambition and primary trust are real questions.
rows.evidence_notes: Long-form business/media presence; outsider brand; consumer/business specificity.
rows.liability_notes: Not a conventional Democrat; office ambition unclear; primary trust barrier.
rows.catalyst_note: Explicit interest, viral long-form interviews, outsider/business lane demand, and materially specific consumer or economic policy hooks.

rows.note: Reportedly gearing up; probably not viable but reliably appears on lists.
rows.vibes_rationale: Authentic about who he is, unfortunately; old-war-room insider is wrong for the moment.
rows.evidence_notes: Long public record; reported ambition; known operator persona.
rows.liability_notes: Old-war-room insider brand; combative without renewal story; probably wrong for the moment.
rows.catalyst_note: Reported gearing-up becoming real, donor lane formation, and some fresh why-now rationale beyond old insider combat experience.

rows.note: ESPN/media personality floating political interest; name ID and combat-media skill, but no party or governing base.
rows.vibes_rationale: High-filter media combat and authentic voice, but the positive governing story and policy specificity are thin.
rows.evidence_notes: Large media platform; repeated public political commentary; outsider celebrity profile.
rows.liability_notes: No elected record, no visible Democratic organization, entertainer-to-candidate trust barrier, and unclear seriousness.
rows.catalyst_note: Explicit run/pass signal, donor or adviser attachment, early-state travel, and whether media attention converts into actual Democratic primary support.

rows.note: Vice president and default Trump-heir lane; strongest machine case, but Rubio chatter has become a live stress test.
rows.vibes_rationale: Authentic factional voice with a clear pugilist story, but broader optimism and coalition expansion remain weak points.
rows.evidence_notes: Vice president; RNC finance role; early GOP polling lead in many averages; market favorite on nominee-specific pages.
rows.liability_notes: Approval softness, over-identification with incumbent outcomes, and Rubio comparison risk.
rows.catalyst_note: Midterm barnstorming, Trump endorsement signals, Iowa/NH travel, and any post-midterm launch conversation with Trump.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Default-heir case: VP, RNC finance role, and clearest loyalty/proximity argument. Main risk is Trump keeping Rubio live or refusing to crown anyone early.

rows.note: Secretary of State / national-security portfolio has turned him into the live alternative to Vance.
rows.vibes_rationale: High fluency and institutional polish; the question is whether MAGA voters read him as heir, rival, or running mate.
rows.evidence_notes: Recent market climb; AtlasIntel Rubio surge; administration visibility and donor chatter.
rows.liability_notes: Could be boxed into VP/elder-statesman role; old 2016 anti-Trump residue; needs permission structure from Trump world.
rows.catalyst_note: Trump public temperature checks, foreign-policy spotlight cycles, donor organization, and whether Vance/Rubio becomes explicit rivalry.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Live alternative case: administration visibility, disciplined loyalty posture, and donor/media momentum. Needs clear Trump permission because old 2016 residue and Vance-default status still matter.

rows.note: Term-limited Florida governor with a real 2024 apparatus scar; still the most obvious non-administration reboot.
rows.vibes_rationale: Material governing record is strong, but the 2024 campaign exposed warmth, long-form, and charisma limits.
rows.evidence_notes: Florida executive record; prior national campaign; single-digit but persistent market pricing.
rows.liability_notes: Already lost the anti-Trump-without-breaking-with-Trump lane; awkward if Vance and Rubio both run.
rows.catalyst_note: Florida post-governor positioning, book/tour choices, and whether he can wait out a Vance/Rubio collision.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Near-zero endorsement case: the 2024 rivalry, Trump's personal grievance style, and DeSantis's attempt to inherit the movement without permission make a direct Trump blessing highly unlikely.

rows.note: Name-ID heir with online-faction pull; more likely kingmaker or media force than disciplined nominee.
rows.vibes_rationale: Authentic to the base and fluent online, but the governing story and campaign discipline are thin.
rows.evidence_notes: Appears in RCP-tracked GOP polling as a double-digit option; market mentions remain low.
rows.liability_notes: Unclear appetite, nepotism fatigue, and high downside if the party wants professionalized succession.
rows.catalyst_note: Any explicit pass/run signal, Trump family positioning, podcast/media tour intensity, and whether he attacks or blesses Vance/Rubio.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Family-name path exists and relationship evidence matters more than polling here, but a formal run still looks less likely than kingmaker/media influence. Chance rises only if he signals real appetite.

rows.note: Cabinet-adjacent outsider brand; mostly a chaos or lane-fracture watchpoint rather than a normal nomination path.
rows.vibes_rationale: High authenticity and heterodox appeal, but message coherence and party fit are unstable.
rows.evidence_notes: Shows up in some GOP preference lists; cross-partisan name ID.
rows.liability_notes: Party fit, administrative baggage, and unpredictable coalition shape.
rows.catalyst_note: Health/agency fights, Trump relationship, and whether anti-establishment Republicans treat him as useful pressure or actual nominee.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Useful heterodox ally, weak party-fit case. Track agency/health fights and whether Trump treats him as a movement partner rather than a GOP nominee.

rows.note: Known Iowa-capable conservative operator; probably more ceiling than floor.
rows.vibes_rationale: Clear ideological combat and debate skill, but low warmth and old-brand fatigue limit expansion.
rows.evidence_notes: 2016 runner-up infrastructure memory; recurring low-single-digit polling.
rows.liability_notes: Personal favorability drag; hard to see Trump-world permission against Vance/Rubio.
rows.catalyst_note: Iowa religious-conservative testing, Senate posture, and whether a foreign-policy or constitutional fight gives him a lane.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Known conservative operator, but low warmth and 2016 history make him a long-shot recipient unless the succession race collapses.

rows.note: Anti-chaos / old-GOP option with residual 2024 donor network; difficult path unless the Trump succession breaks badly.
rows.vibes_rationale: Polished and clear, but her coalition is too anti-MAGA-coded for the current primary without a major failure cascade.
rows.evidence_notes: 2024 campaign; early-state name ID; low but measurable polling.
rows.liability_notes: Base trust problem; hard to reenter if party treats 2024 as settled.
rows.catalyst_note: A Vance/Rubio collapse, donor panic, foreign-policy crisis, or early New Hampshire polls that reopen the anti-chaos lane.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Residual 2024 opposition lane and base-trust problem make a Trump endorsement close to zero without a major party shock.

rows.note: Media-native insurgent who can pressure the discourse even if the nomination path is narrow.
rows.vibes_rationale: Very high long-form energy and ideological specificity; also high performative-combat risk.
rows.evidence_notes: 2024 debate/media profile; recurring low-single-digit polling.
rows.liability_notes: Novelty fade, executive-record gap, and crowded MAGA successor lane.
rows.catalyst_note: Podcast/long-form breakouts, Ohio or administration positioning, and whether he becomes attack dog against one of the top two.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Loyal media-native pressure candidate. More plausible as surrogate or attack dog unless Trump wants a disruptive non-Vance/non-Rubio option.

rows.note: Market-visible media figure and factional pressure point; has publicly denied interest, so this is mostly tail-risk tracking.
rows.vibes_rationale: Dominant long-form media presence and anti-establishment clarity, but candidate discipline and actual appetite are unproven.
rows.evidence_notes: Market pages keep him in the mix despite denial; low polling when listed.
rows.liability_notes: No campaign structure, explicit non-run signals, and hard ceiling with institutional Republicans.
rows.catalyst_note: Run/pass restatements, feud with administration figures, and whether markets keep pricing media influence as candidacy risk.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Factional media influence is real; formal candidate appetite and Trump permission are both thin.

rows.note: Virginia governor sleeper profile; plausible only if the party wants electability and cultural conservatism without full MAGA theater.
rows.vibes_rationale: Suburban cultural-conservative story is coherent, but national charisma and MAGA permission are untested.
rows.evidence_notes: Virginia executive profile; occasional polling/market listing.
rows.liability_notes: Low national profile and no obvious lane if Vance/Rubio remain healthy.
rows.catalyst_note: Virginia/GOP donor chatter, education/culture-war reprise, and post-midterm appetite for a governor instead of administration succession.
rows.trump_endorsement_note: Governor/electability lane needs a party mood shift and explicit Trump tolerance; currently more donor fantasy than succession favorite.

vibes_check.definition: Patriotic repair: optimistic about America, emotionally normal, clear-eyed without doom, picks fights against systems/abusers/Trumpism rather than voters, and can survive long-form unscripted media without feeling synthetic.
vibes_check.note: Shown beside, not inside, the market/poll model. The schema preserves the component disagreement underneath the single displayed bar.
vibes_check.hypothesis: If the thesis is right, higher Vibes Check candidates should begin to improve in polling and/or markets as voters pay closer attention.
vibes_check.label: Subjective read on patriotic-repair affect, derived from component scores and confidence notes.

vibes_model.score_field: derived at render time from vibes_dimensions

dimensions.hope_story: Can they tell a positive future-facing story?
dimensions.material_specificity: Are there concrete policies/examples under the story?
dimensions.authentic_longform: Can they hold up in low-filter long-form settings?
dimensions.problem_clarity: Can they name what is broken without sounding doom-only?
dimensions.fight_selection: Do they pick fights that clarify the story instead of performing combat?

vibes_model.note: The displayed vibes_score is a weighted average of the five stored ingredients, not a hand-entered primary field.

catalyst_model.note: Catalysts are tracked as a calendar: dated or seasonal windows where market and polling snapshots are most likely to deserve a fresh read.
catalyst_model.automation_note: Manual watch for now; these dates are shaped so scheduled refresh jobs can be added later.

calendar.title: Earned-media and staff tells
calendar.detail: Big speeches, viral interviews, donor/consultant leaks, PAC or staff moves, and early-state travel. Markets usually move before polls here.
calendar.cadence: Sample when a candidate gets an obvious breakout or pass signal.

calendar.title: GA / PA primary gate
calendar.detail: Georgia and Pennsylvania federal primaries make ambition and ballot-path constraints more legible for Ossoff, Shapiro, and adjacent lanes.
calendar.cadence: Refresh after results; watch Georgia runoff path if needed.

calendar.title: Fall campaign stress test
calendar.detail: Live-cycle governors and senators get tested on message discipline, fundraising, TV performance, and whether their lane looks exportable.
calendar.cadence: Snapshot on major debates, polling breaks, or high-salience ads.

calendar.title: Midterm reset
calendar.detail: The biggest repricing window. Winners, losers, and overperformers shape what the party thinks the post-2024 lesson should be.
calendar.cadence: Refresh market, poll, and mention baselines within the first week after election night.

calendar.title: Future-of-party tour
calendar.detail: Watch who gives major speeches, does Sunday shows, gets donor meetings, and starts visiting early states.
calendar.cadence: Capture explicit travel, PAC, book, and donor signals.

calendar.title: Exploratory phase
calendar.detail: Exploratory committees, book launches, PAC moves, staff hires, and first serious national polling can move low-name-ID candidates into visibility.
calendar.cadence: Monthly baseline unless a candidate files, passes, or hires senior staff.

calendar.title: DNC and cattle-call calendar
calendar.detail: The DNC early-state map and first cattle calls make regional advantages, favorability, and coalition tests more concrete.
calendar.cadence: Refresh when the early-state order or major cattle-call schedule becomes concrete.

calendar.title: Debates and voting
calendar.detail: Debates and actual voting are the classic reset window: communicators rise and paper candidates get exposed.
calendar.cadence: Move from periodic snapshots to event-by-event updates.

mention_model.note: Manual-first mention tracking. Capture accessible articles, clips, newsletters, forums, search/news results, and public pages; label each mention good, bad, neutral, or unclear; keep raw refs for audit.
mention_model.hypothesis: Useful mention signal should lead market or poll movement by at least one later snapshot. Same-week mirroring is descriptive, not predictive.

fields.sources: List of source buckets used in this capture.
fields.examples: Representative refs/snippets, kept short and linkable.
fields.confidence: pending, low, medium, or high based on volume and source diversity.

Democratic.note: Catalysts are tracked as a calendar: dated or seasonal windows where market and polling snapshots are most likely to deserve a fresh read.
Democratic.automation_note: Manual watch for now; these dates are shaped so scheduled refresh jobs can be added later.

calendar.title: Earned-media and staff tells
calendar.detail: Big speeches, viral interviews, donor/consultant leaks, PAC or staff moves, and early-state travel. Markets usually move before polls here.
calendar.cadence: Sample when a candidate gets an obvious breakout or pass signal.

calendar.title: GA / PA primary gate
calendar.detail: Georgia and Pennsylvania federal primaries make ambition and ballot-path constraints more legible for Ossoff, Shapiro, and adjacent lanes.
calendar.cadence: Refresh after results; watch Georgia runoff path if needed.

calendar.title: Fall campaign stress test
calendar.detail: Live-cycle governors and senators get tested on message discipline, fundraising, TV performance, and whether their lane looks exportable.
calendar.cadence: Snapshot on major debates, polling breaks, or high-salience ads.

calendar.title: Midterm reset
calendar.detail: The biggest repricing window. Winners, losers, and overperformers shape what the party thinks the post-2024 lesson should be.
calendar.cadence: Refresh market, poll, and mention baselines within the first week after election night.

calendar.title: Future-of-party tour
calendar.detail: Watch who gives major speeches, does Sunday shows, gets donor meetings, and starts visiting early states.
calendar.cadence: Capture explicit travel, PAC, book, and donor signals.

calendar.title: Exploratory phase
calendar.detail: Exploratory committees, book launches, PAC moves, staff hires, and first serious national polling can move low-name-ID candidates into visibility.
calendar.cadence: Monthly baseline unless a candidate files, passes, or hires senior staff.

calendar.title: DNC and cattle-call calendar
calendar.detail: The DNC early-state map and first cattle calls make regional advantages, favorability, and coalition tests more concrete.
calendar.cadence: Refresh when the early-state order or major cattle-call schedule becomes concrete.

calendar.title: Debates and voting
calendar.detail: Debates and actual voting are the classic reset window: communicators rise and paper candidates get exposed.
calendar.cadence: Move from periodic snapshots to event-by-event updates.

Republican.note: Republican catalysts are tracked as a succession calendar: Trump relationship and endorsement probability first, then 2026 midterm ownership, early-state tells, polling, and markets.
Republican.automation_note: Manual watch for now; relationship sources lead this metric, while polls and markets are confirmation signals.

calendar.title: Trump-heir temperature checks
calendar.detail: Watch public praise, nicknames, humiliation, feud temperature, reconciliation reporting, adviser leaks, and whether Trump keeps Vance and Rubio in managed rivalry.
calendar.cadence: Refresh when Trump explicitly names, praises, tests, humiliates, forgives, or undercuts a successor.

calendar.title: Midterm ownership tour
calendar.detail: Vance has RNC finance and barnstorming duties; Rubio has administration visibility. Both get judged against the midterm outcome.
calendar.cadence: Snapshot after major Iowa/NH/SC/NV visits, fundraising reports, or national surrogate moments.

calendar.title: GOP midterm reset
calendar.detail: If Republicans overperform, the heir to Trump gets stronger. If they underperform, donors and media may reopen the successor question.
calendar.cadence: Refresh market, poll, and mention baselines in the week after election night.

calendar.title: Pass/run and staffing tells
calendar.detail: Senior consultants, super PAC posture, book/media plans, and donor networks clarify whether the race is Vance-default, Rubio-live, or wider.
calendar.cadence: Capture senior-hire leaks, PAC filings, donor meetings, and explicit deferrals.

calendar.title: Carve-out state circuit
calendar.detail: Republican early-state gravity still runs through Iowa, New Hampshire, South Carolina, and Nevada unless rules change.
calendar.cadence: Refresh when the RNC calendar is concrete or candidates start repeated visits.

calendar.title: Debates and first contests
calendar.detail: The succession story meets voters: Vance tests heir status, Rubio tests crossover inside MAGA, and governors/media figures test whether there is room.
calendar.cadence: Move from periodic snapshots to event-by-event updates.

source_links.label: AP: former Trump critics as VP hopefuls
ABC: Trump and DeSantis bury-the-hatchet report
NBC: Trump pressure on disloyal Republicans

trump_endorsement_model.definition: Current subjective probability that Trump gives this candidate the meaningful pre-primary endorsement, blessing, or permission structure before the GOP field settles.
trump_endorsement_model.scale: 0-100 percent. Candidate probabilities are candidate-level monitoring estimates, not a poll. Polling and markets are secondary confirmation; relationship evidence and Trump-world behavior lead the update.
update_triggers: Explicit Truth Social posts, rally remarks, nicknames, praise, or public humiliation patterns
Reporting on Trump grievances, reconciliation attempts, private calls, dinners, and adviser temperature checks
Joint appearances, delegated surrogate roles, cabinet/administration proximity, and who gets protected or undercut
FEC/PAC money, donor bundling, senior-staff movement, and super PAC posture tied to Trump-world permission
Polling or prediction-market breaks only when they coincide with relationship or loyalty evidence
trump_endorsement_model.caution: Keep this separate from nominee probability: Trump can bless one candidate, delay endorsement, float several names, or use praise tactically. Do not raise a rival just because they poll well if the personal relationship evidence points the other way.

early_state_calendar.title: Dem Early Calendar Tracker
early_state_calendar.summary: Assumes New Hampshire and Nevada are locked early. The remaining calendar signal is the Midwest slot, currently Illinois / Iowa / Michigan, and the South slot, currently Georgia / North Carolina / South Carolina / Tennessee / Virginia. Use this as a market trigger map when the DNC narrows or sets the schedule.

regions.name: Midwest slot
regions.read: Main axis: Whitmer/Slotkin home-state Michigan vs Pritzker/Emanuel home-state Illinois vs old-school retail Iowa.

states.effect: Biggest boost to Whitmer; Slotkin up; also useful for Shapiro, Buttigieg, Gallego, and Kelly because it tests unions, Detroit Black voters, Arab/Muslim voters, college suburbs, and industrial working-class messaging. Pritzker loses the home-state jackpot.

Big boost to Pritzker and Emanuel. Also friendlier to Harris, Booker, AOC, and Newsom than Iowa because Chicago/suburbs make it diverse, urban, donor/media-heavy, and college-heavy. Whitmer and Slotkin lose their cleanest calendar catalyst.

Boosts Buttigieg, Beshear, Walz, Polis, and retail-campaign unknowns. Downgrades Harris, AOC, Booker, Warnock, and Moore because it recreates the older, whiter, less representative early-state problem.

regions.name: South slot
regions.read: Main axis: Georgia growth/swing-state coalition vs South Carolina older Black establishment vs North Carolina hybrid suburbs/Black electorate/rural test.

states.effect: Warnock and Ossoff get the biggest direct boost. Harris, Booker, Moore, AOC, and Gallego also benefit from Atlanta metro, younger Black voters, college suburbs, Asian/Latino growth, and swing-state electability.

Cooper and Stein get the largest home-state boost. Also helps Beshear, Kelly, Gallego, Shapiro, and Buttigieg-style swing/suburban candidates. Harris, Booker, and Moore remain fine, but it is less clean for them than South Carolina.

Boosts Harris, Booker, Moore, and Warnock through older Black Democratic, church, and establishment networks. Downgrades Buttigieg, Newsom, Polis, and Cuban unless they build real Black institutional support.

Boosts suburban, moderate, technocratic, security-state candidates; helpful to Spanberger-style profiles, Moore regionally, and Buttigieg-ish technocrats. Black-church and rural-populist lanes lose some force.

Oddball pick. Boosts Beshear and Walz-ish rural/moderate/Appalachian/Southern white-working-class appeal. Downgrades most national diverse-coalition candidates and weakens the argument that the early South slot should center Black Democratic voters.

combinations.effect: Whitmer and Warnock/Ossoff up; Harris, Booker, Moore, AOC, and Gallego also up. Pritzker down. Best future-coalition/swing-state signal.
Whitmer up plus Harris, Booker, Moore, and Warnock up. Warnock gets less than with Georgia. Newsom and Pritzker weaker.
Pritzker/Emanuel up and Harris/Booker/Moore up. Whitmer/Slotkin down; Buttigieg/Beshear retail lane weaker.
Buttigieg/Beshear/Walz/Polis retail lane up, while Harris/Booker/Moore retain SC protection. Whitmer and Pritzker lose home-state boosts.
Pritzker plus Warnock/Ossoff is the clearest dual home-field outcome. Diverse/urban/media candidates up; rural and retail candidates down.
Whitmer/Slotkin plus Cooper/Stein up. Also helps Shapiro, Buttigieg, Kelly, Gallego, and Beshear-style swing/suburban candidates.
Strong signal that DNC wants retail/rural/white-working-class testing back in the early window. Beshear/Walz/Buttigieg-style candidates up; diverse-coalition candidates down.

candidate_impacts.best: South Carolina, then Georgia; Michigan or Illinois in the Midwest.
candidate_impacts.worst: Iowa plus Tennessee, or a South slot that bypasses Black establishment power.
candidate_impacts.read: SC is a real boost because it protects the older Black institutional lane; NC is okay but less clean.

candidate_impacts.best: Georgia or North Carolina; Illinois or Michigan.
candidate_impacts.read: Needs diverse metro/suburban terrain. White retail states and SC institutional tests make the California-polish liability louder.
Young, diverse, labor, Latino, and urban/progressive density helps. SC is not fatal but older establishment Black electorate is not her cleanest test.
Swing-state suburban and working-class terrain helps more than a pure party-base test.
NH already helps. Iowa revives the retail lane; SC reopens the Black-voter weakness story.

candidate_impacts.worst: Michigan or Iowa plus South Carolina.
candidate_impacts.read: Illinois is the jackpot but can look favorite-son. Losing Illinois is a direct downgrade.

candidate_impacts.worst: South Carolina, North Carolina, or Tennessee.
candidate_impacts.read: Georgia early is his cleanest calendar catalyst; any other South pick makes him more generic.

candidate_impacts.best: South Carolina or North Carolina; Michigan or Illinois.
candidate_impacts.read: Black electorate plus service/governor biography is the fit, with NC as a regional hybrid.
Wants white working-class/moderate Democrats and rural credibility, not a purely metro-progressive calendar.

candidate_impacts.best: Nevada fixed, plus Georgia or North Carolina and Michigan.
candidate_impacts.read: Veteran + Latino + swing-state story needs diverse swing electorates; old-school SC is less direct.
NC early makes him real; other South picks leave him as a generic low-name-ID governor.
Military/suburban competence calendar is the fit; SC does not naturally amplify him.
Georgia early is a major boost; SC still helps through Black church and establishment networks.
Michigan early is probably the biggest single Midwest boost for any candidate.

candidate_impacts.best: Michigan; Iowa as a secondary Midwest fit.
candidate_impacts.read: Michigan turns her from profile-fit dark horse into a live calendar story.

candidate_impacts.best: Illinois or Michigan; Virginia or North Carolina.
candidate_impacts.read: Needs suburban, professional-class, competence-liberal terrain. SC is neutral-to-soft.
Best with NH/Iowa-style high-information electorates; weak in Black-establishment tests.

candidate_impacts.best: Nevada fixed, plus Illinois/Michigan and Georgia/North Carolina.
candidate_impacts.read: Needs diverse, younger, high-information Democratic electorates.

candidate_impacts.best: South Carolina or Georgia; Illinois or Michigan.
candidate_impacts.read: Charisma plus church/civic coalition benefits from Black electorate states.
Rural/teacher/vernacular Midwest affect wants retail and working-class exposure.
NC early is his huge catalyst; otherwise he needs donor/media proof of why-now.

candidate_impacts.best: Illinois, Michigan, Georgia, North Carolina, or Virginia.
candidate_impacts.read: Needs big media markets and suburban/business outsider voters; party-trust tests expose weakness.
Urban operator credibility needs IL. Leftier or Black-establishment tests make his downside louder.

candidate_impacts.best: Illinois or Virginia; Nevada already helps a little through media-aware diverse electorates.
candidate_impacts.read: Needs big media markets and high-information voters willing to entertain an outsider. Retail, Black-establishment, or rural tests expose lack of party structure.
